pages.title: Contact Me - IAN LAVERY MP
headings: h2: IAN LAVERY MP Working for Wansbeck
h2: Contact Me

h2: Please be aware that due to Covid Restrictions and the current guidelines to work from home my office is at limited capacity and may not be available to answer if you call. If nobody answers please leave a message and someone will get back to you as soon as possible, or alternatively send an email.
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me - IAN LAVERY MP Wansbeck Constituency Contact Me Get Involved IAN LAVERY MP Working for Wansbeck About Latest News Parliamentary Activity Contact Me Cost of Living Leaflet Twitter Facebook Instagram Contact Me Am I your MP? Wansbeck Constituency Info Newsletter sign-up WANSBECK CONSTITUENCY OFFICE Tel: 01670 852494 Email: ian.lavery.mp@parliament.uk PARLIAMENTARY OFFICE Tel: 02072197177 Email: ian.lavery.mp@parliament.uk Write to Ian: 7 Esther Court, Ashington, Northumb erland, NE63 8AP Please be aware that due to Covid Restrictions and the current guidelines to work from home my office is at limited capacity and may not be available to answer if you call. If nobody answers please leave a message and someone will get back to you as soon as possible, or alternatively send an email. I am always happy to help constituents with Casework or to hear about an issue that they are concerned about. However, there are times when I am not able to help with certain issues. I am unable to intervene in private disputes between individuals, decisions made by the courts or in the functions of a local authority; for example dustbins, street cleaning, housing repairs or tenancy matters. For local authority matters, please contact your local councillor. If you are unsure who your local councillor is, go to http://committee.northumberland.gov.uk/CouncillorSearch.aspx What I can do is help with matters that relate to central government. This includes Inland Revenue, Immigration, Health (including the NHS and hospitals), Education (but not individual matters within schools), the Department for Work and Pensions and Prisons. This isn’t an exhaustive list, and my helpful and friendly staff will try to direct you to the best place to contact if you aren’t sure of who to contact. Most cases are dealt with by the team in the constituency office, who handle cases from first contact and have extensive local knowledge. If you would like to see one of my caseworkers regarding your case, they will be happy to make an appointment either in my office or as a home visit. The time taken to resolve issues will vary dependent on the complexity of each individual case. However all matters are treated with the strictest confidentiality. Social channels Twitter Facebook Instagram Contact details Email: ian.lavery.mp@parliament.uk Further details 0 ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: About Ian - IAN LAVERY MP
headings: h2: IAN LAVERY MP Working for Wansbeck
h2: About Ian
h2: From Pit to Politics

h3: I have had the honour of representing Wansbeck in Parliament from 2010 & was re-elected in 2015, 2017 and again in 2019. Before I was elected as an MP, I was proud to serve the former Wansbeck District as an elected Councillor. I have called Northumberland home my entire life, and this wonderful County is the only area I would ever want to represent in Parliament.

h3: After I left school, I enrolled on to a Youth Training Scheme in the construction Industry, before gaining employment at Lynemouth Colliery. I later transferred to Ellington Colliery where I was granted a National Coal Board apprenticeship.

h3: As with so many in my generation, I was politicised by the 1984-85 Miners Strike, where I saw how the full power of the state, press and industry was deployed against coalfield communities. During this period I also saw the spirit of comradeship, collectiveness and solidarity that helped keep communities together.

h3: After the strike, I became more active in the Labour and Trade Union movement, going on to become the first Cabinet Chairman of Wansbeck District Council. I became the General Secretary for the National Union of Mineworkers (Northumberland area).
h3: Since becoming an MP I have worked to represent my constituency, especially those who have fallen victim to injustices, across the U.K.

h3: Throughout my career, I have dedicated a great deal of time to voluntary societies, and as a result, I have the honour of holding many honorary positions. I am a Trustee of the Coal Industry Social Welfare Organisation in the North East area, the Hirst Welfare Centre and the Woodhorn Colliery Museum. In addition to these, I am a Patron of Headway for South East Northumberland. I am also Chairman and trustee of the world famous Pitman Painters organisation.

h3: Since the election of Jeremy Corbyn as leader of the Labour Party, I have served as Shadow Cabinet Office Minister and as the Labour Party spokesperson for Trade Unions. Ahead of the 2017 snap General Election, I was asked to be Labour’s joint Elections Coordinator and following the result of the election, I was appointed national Chair of the Labour Party, a position which gives me great pride to serve as.
h3: In my spare time, I thoroughly enjoy sport; in the main football, cricket and boxing.
h3: I married my wife Hilary in 1986 & have two sons, Ian Jnr and Liam.
h3: You can find out more about my work on the Parliament website, and my full voting record is available on the They Work for You website.

I am a Trustee of the Coal Industry Social Welfare Organisation in the North East area, the Hirst Welfare Centre and the Woodhorn Colliery Museum. In addition to these, I am a Patron of Headway for South East Northumberland. I am also Chairman and trustee of the world famous Pitman Painters organisation. Since the election of Jeremy Corbyn as leader of the Labour Party, I have served as Shadow Cabinet Office Minister and as the Labour Party spokesperson for Trade Unions. Ahead of the 2017 snap General Election, I was asked to be Labour’s joint Elections Coordinator and following the result of the election, I was appointed national Chair of the Labour Party, a position which gives me great pride to serve as.
